Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The prompt describes a process but does not provide explicit constraints

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt says construct a Python dictionary representing the Log Skeleton for the given scenario It does not give explicit constraints in bullet points

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to infer constraints from the description

Name: Validation - PE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Am I supposed to invent reasonable constraints based on the description

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Is it possible that the prompt is originally from a test where a table or list of constraints was given but omitted here

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should state my assumptions and provide a reasonable log skeleton based on a typical sequential process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a typical order fulfillment activities are mostly sequential

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can define Always Before CA before RI RI before PI PI before QC QC before PO PO before GSL GSL before D and maybe IC before RP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences each activity typically occurs exactly once per case

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given the lack of specific constraints the most generic answer might be to set all sets empty except activ freq but the scenario implies a sequence so I should provide a plausible answer

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Once availability is confirmed items are reserved This suggests CA must occur before RI

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The picked items are inspected after picking packed after quality check label after packing dispatch after label and payment after invoice

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
